display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Geometry & Topology
(vertices, constraining segments,
planar constraining facets)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Discretize Edges & Surfaces
Sample vertices along edges
and on surface facets
(respects curved CAD geometry
via parametric evaluation)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Extract Constraints
Segments → subsegment list
Facets → independent 2D Delaunay
triangulations per facet
(maintained separately from
the tetrahedralization)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Create Bounding Tetrahedron
Encloses all input vertices
with large margin

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Find Conflicting Tetrahedra
1. Circumsphere test: is point
inside tet's circumsphere?
2. BFS flood fill from seed tet
(no constraint-crossing yet —
constraints not recovered)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Extract Cavity Boundary
Triangular faces appearing once
= boundary of the cavity

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Retriangulate Cavity
New tetrahedra from each
boundary face to inserted vertex

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Missing subsegment detected
(if missing → it is encroached,
by Delaunay property)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Split subsegment at midpoint
- Insert midpoint via Bowyer-Watson
- Two new subsegments created
- Recurse if either is still missing

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Identify missing subfacets
Compare each facet's independent
2D Delaunay triangulation against
faces of the tetrahedralization

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Projection Lemma check
If vertex p encroaches subfacet f
but proj_F(p) lies outside facet F
→ p also encroaches a boundary
subsegment → split that first

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Split encroached subfacet
at its circumcenter
- Insert into both the facet
triangulation AND the
tetrahedralization
- But if circumcenter would
encroach a subsegment,
split that subsegment instead

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Remove exterior tetrahedra
Tetrahedra outside the PLC
boundary (in convex hull but
outside facet-bounded region)
are removed BEFORE refinement
→ prevents spurious small angles

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Split encroached subsegment
at midpoint via Bowyer-Watson
(diametral sphere test:
vertex inside smallest sphere
enclosing the subsegment)

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
Split encroached subfacet
at circumcenter
(equatorial sphere test:
non-coplanar vertex inside
smallest sphere through
the 3 subfacet vertices)

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Quality check
Circumradius / shortest edge > B
(B ≥ 2 for guaranteed termination)
Process worst ratio first

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
Insert circumcenter
via Bowyer-Watson
→ eliminates skinny tet
(its circumsphere is
no longer empty)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Sliver Removal (heuristic)
Slivers have good circumradius-to-
edge ratio but near-zero volume
and extreme dihedral angles
(near 0° or 180°)
→ Split at circumcenter targeting
dihedral angle bounds
→ No theoretical guarantee,
but effective in practice

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Mesh Smoothing & Optimization
(optional)
- Laplacian / optimization-based
vertex smoothing
- Topological transforms
(2-3, 3-2 flips)
- Removes remaining slivers

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
